title: Formal Requirements and Filing Period for Complaints under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content: The formal requirements of a complaint include: 1) Name(s) and address(es) of the complainant(s); 2) Name(s) and address(es) of the respondent(s); 3) Specific act(s) or omission(s) constituting the alleged offense; 4) Place where the offense was committed; 5) Date when the offense was committed; 6) The amount of claim, if any; 7) The relief sought. The complaint must be in writing and under oath. The Administration may initiate recruitment violation and disciplinary action cases on its own initiative based on official reports, including those from labor attaches and ambassadors worldwide. Complaints against recruitment agencies, foreign employers, and workers must be filed within three (3) years from the time the cause of action accrued.
